# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController

    def search
        @products = Product.all

        if params[:search]
          search = params[:search]
          
          if search[:name].present?
            @products = @products.where("name LIKE ?", "%#{search[:name]}%")
          end
          
          if search[:category].present?
            @products = @products.where("category LIKE ?", "%#{search[:category]}%")
          end

          if search[:min_price].present? && search[:max_price].present?
            @products = @products.where("price BETWEEN ? AND ?", search[:min_price], search[:max_price])
          elsif search[:min_price].present?
            @products = @products.where("price >= ?", search[:min_price])
          elsif search[:max_price].present?
            @products = @products.where("price <= ?", search[:max_price])
          end
          
        end

        render :index
    end
end
